text: This report assesses the quality of advocacy in proceedings in the youth court and the key elements of advocacy that will enable the Bar Standard Board to determine whether any regulatory intervention is required to improve the quality of youth advocacy. The study consisted of a survey of 215 advocates, interviews with 96 stakeholders (including advocates, young people, youth court magistrates and court-based YOT workers) and observations in 4 youth courts and 5 Crown Courts across England and Wales. The work of advocates in the youth court cannot be understood as a discrete entity; it has to be understood in relation to the broader legal, institutional and cultural context of the justice system and must be considered in relation to a number of recommendations to improve the system as a whole, contained within the report. In addition, the report includes recommendations regarding court-based facilitators of advocacy and concerning the learning and training of advocates.
